papers.title: Reasoning Structure Matters for Safety Alignment of Reasoning Models
papers.abstract: Large reasoning models (LRMs) achieve strong performance on complex reasoning tasks but often generate harmful responses to malicious user queries. This paper investigates the underlying cause of these safety risks and shows that the issue lies in the reasoning structure itself. Based on this insight, we claim that effective safety alignment can be achieved by altering the reasoning structure. We propose AltTrain, a simple yet effective post training method that explicitly alters the reasoning structure of LRMs. AltTrain is both practical and generalizable, requiring no complex reinforcement learning (RL) training or reward design, only supervised finetuning (SFT) with a lightweight 1K training examples. Experiments across LRM backbones and model sizes demonstrate strong safety alignment, along with robust generalization across reasoning, QA, summarization, and multilingual setting.

analysis.tldr: 通过改变推理结构实现推理模型的安全对齐，仅需轻量SFT。
analysis.research_motivation: 大型推理模型在复杂推理任务上表现优异，但容易对恶意用户查询生成有害响应，现有安全对齐方法复杂且依赖强化学习。
analysis.problem: 推理模型的安全风险源于其推理结构本身，需要一种简单有效的方法来改变推理结构以实现安全对齐。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要指出推理模型的安全风险与推理结构直接相关，但未提供具体失败模式或瓶颈分析。
analysis.method: 提出AltTrain，一种后训练方法，通过监督微调（SFT）显式改变推理模型的推理结构，仅需1000个轻量训练样本。
contributions: 揭示了推理模型安全风险源于推理结构本身。
提出AltTrain，一种简单、实用且可泛化的安全对齐方法，无需复杂强化学习或奖励设计。
analysis.experiments: 在多种推理模型骨干和模型规模上实验，展示了强安全对齐效果，并在推理、问答、摘要和多语言设置中具有鲁棒泛化能力。摘要未提供具体数值。
limitations: 仅依赖SFT，可能对极端复杂攻击的鲁棒性不足。
1000样本的轻量训练可能无法覆盖所有安全场景。
analysis.primary_area_en: alignment, fairness, safety, privacy, and societal considerations
analysis.reason: 方法简单有效，针对推理模型安全对齐这一重要问题，实验显示泛化能力强，但摘要未提供具体数值。
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 通过改变推理结构实现推理模型的安全对齐，仅需轻量SFT。

papers.title: Personalized Benchmarking: Evaluating LLMs by Individual Preferences
papers.abstract: With the rise in capabilities of large language models (LLMs) and their deployment in real-world tasks, evaluating LLM alignment with human preferences has become an important challenge. Current benchmarks average preferences across all users to compute aggregate ratings, overlooking individual user preferences when establishing model rankings. Since users have varying preferences in different contexts, we call for personalized LLM benchmarks that rank models according to individual needs. We compute personalized model rankings using ELO ratings and Bradley-Terry coefficients for 115 active Chatbot Arena users and analyze how user query characteristics (topics and writing style) relate to LLM ranking variations. We demonstrate that individual rankings of LLM models diverge dramatically from aggregate LLM rankings, with Bradley-Terry correlations averaging only $ρ= 0.04$ (57\% of users show near-zero or negative correlation) and ELO ratings showing moderate correlation ($ρ= 0.43$). Through topic modeling and style analysis, we find users exhibit substantial heterogeneity in topical interests and communication styles, influencing their model preferences. We further show that a compact combination of topic and style features provides a useful feature space for predicting user-specific model rankings. Our results provide strong quantitative evidence that aggregate benchmarks fail to capture individual preferences for most users, and highlight the importance of developing personalized benchmarks that rank LLM models according to individual user preferences.

analysis.tldr: 个性化基准测试揭示聚合排名无法反映大多数用户的个人偏好，需转向个性化LLM评估。
analysis.research_motivation: 当前LLM基准测试通过聚合所有用户偏好计算平均评分，忽略了个体差异，导致模型排名无法满足个性化需求。
analysis.problem: 如何量化个体用户对LLM的偏好差异，并构建个性化模型排名。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 个体排名与聚合排名存在显著差异：Bradley-Terry相关系数平均仅0.04，57%用户呈近零或负相关；ELO评分中等相关（ρ=0.43）。用户查询主题和写作风格异质性影响偏好。
analysis.method: 基于Chatbot Arena 115名活跃用户的交互数据，使用ELO评分和Bradley-Terry系数计算个性化排名，并通过主题建模和风格分析预测用户特定排名。
contributions: 首次系统量化个体LLM排名与聚合排名的差异，揭示聚合基准的局限性。
提出结合主题和风格特征预测用户偏好的方法，为个性化基准提供特征空间。
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果，但提及分析了115名用户、主题建模和风格分析。
limitations: 仅基于Chatbot Arena用户，可能不具广泛代表性。
未探讨个性化基准的实际构建与验证。

analysis.one_sentence_summary: 个性化基准测试揭示聚合排名无法反映大多数用户的个人偏好，需转向个性化LLM评估。

papers.title: Localization-Guided Foreground Augmentation in Autonomous Driving
papers.abstract: Autonomous driving systems often degrade under adverse visibility conditions-such as rain, nighttime, or snow-where online scene geometry (e.g., lane dividers, road boundaries, and pedestrian crossings) becomes sparse or fragmented. While high-definition (HD) maps can provide missing structural context, they are costly to construct and maintain at scale. We propose Localization-Guided Foreground Augmentation (LG-FA), a lightweight and plug-and-play inference module that enhances foreground perception by enriching geometric context online. LG-FA: (i) incrementally constructs a sparse global vector layer from per-frame Bird's-Eye View (BEV) predictions; (ii) estimates ego pose via class-constrained geometric alignment, jointly improving localization and completing missing local topology; and (iii) reprojects the augmented foreground into a unified global frame to improve per-frame predictions. Experiments on challenging nuScenes sequences demonstrate that LG-FA improves the geometric completeness and temporal stability of BEV representations, reduces localization error, and produces globally consistent lane and topology reconstructions. The module can be seamlessly integrated into existing BEV-based perception systems without backbone modification. By providing a reliable geometric context prior, LG-FA enhances temporal consistency and supplies stable structural support for downstream modules such as tracking and decision-making.

analysis.tldr: 提出一种轻量级在线模块LG-FA，通过构建稀疏全局向量层和类约束几何对齐，增强自动驾驶中恶劣可见度条件下的前景感知。
analysis.research_motivation: 自动驾驶系统在雨、夜、雪等恶劣可见度条件下，在线场景几何结构（如车道线、路边界）变得稀疏或碎片化，导致感知性能下降。高精地图虽能提供缺失的结构上下文，但构建和维护成本高昂。
analysis.problem: 如何在不依赖高精地图的前提下，在线增强恶劣条件下的前景感知几何完整性和时间稳定性。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 恶劣可见度条件下，在线场景几何结构稀疏或碎片化，导致BEV表示不完整、时间不稳定，且定位误差增大。
analysis.method: LG-FA模块：(i) 从逐帧BEV预测中增量构建稀疏全局向量层；(ii) 通过类约束几何对齐估计自车位姿，联合改进定位并补全缺失局部拓扑；(iii) 将增强的前景重投影到统一全局帧以改进逐帧预测。
contributions: 提出一种轻量级、即插即用的推理模块LG-FA，无需修改骨干网络即可增强BEV感知的几何完整性和时间稳定性。
通过类约束几何对齐联合优化定位和局部拓扑补全，减少定位误差并生成全局一致的车道和拓扑重建。
analysis.experiments: 在nuScenes数据集上验证，LG-FA提升了BEV表示的几何完整性和时间稳定性，降低了定位误差，并生成了全局一致的车道和拓扑重建。摘要未提供具体数值。
limitations: 可能依赖BEV预测的质量，在极端恶劣条件下预测本身可能不准确。
增量构建全局向量层可能引入累积误差。
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to computer vision, audio, language, and other modalities
analysis.reason: 方法轻量且即插即用，但实验信息有限，未提供具体数值，适合保存后续关注。
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出一种轻量级在线模块LG-FA，通过构建稀疏全局向量层和类约束几何对齐，增强自动驾驶中恶劣可见度条件下的前景感知。

papers.title: Fine-Tuning Small Reasoning Models for Quantum Field Theory
papers.abstract: Despite the growing application of Large Language Models (LLMs) to theoretical physics, there is little academic exploration into how domain-specific physics reasoning ability develops while training these models. To investigate this, we perform the first academic fine-tuning study of small (7B-parameter) reasoning models dedicated specifically to theoretical physics. Because open-source verifiable training data required to train such capabilities is scarce, we developed a robust data generation pipeline that can both create synthetic problems and make existing human-authored problems suitable for model training. Selecting Quantum Field Theory (QFT) as our primary domain, we generated over 2,500 synthetic problems alongside a curated collection of human-adapted problems sourced from arXiv and standard pedagogical resources. We conduct both Reinforcement Learning (RL) and Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) experiments, benchmarking performance gains as well as generalization to other physics domains. We perform an extensive analysis of model chains-of-though before and after fine-tuning, to understand how reasoning errors evolve during RL and SFT. Finally, we publicly release our data pipeline, verifiable QFT training data, and $\sim$200M tokens of QFT reasoning traces.

analysis.tldr: 首个针对量子场论微调小推理模型的研究，开发数据生成管线并分析推理错误演化。
analysis.research_motivation: 大语言模型在理论物理中的应用日益增长，但领域特定物理推理能力如何通过训练发展缺乏学术探索，且开源可验证训练数据稀缺。
analysis.problem: 如何为理论物理（特别是量子场论）生成可验证训练数据，并通过微调小推理模型提升其物理推理能力，同时分析推理错误的变化。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 开发数据生成管线，合成超过2500个量子场论问题并改编arXiv和教材中的人类问题；使用强化学习和监督微调对7B参数推理模型进行微调。
contributions: 首次对专门用于理论物理的小推理模型进行学术微调研究。
开发了可生成可验证训练数据的数据管线，并公开了量子场论训练数据和推理轨迹。
analysis.experiments: 在量子场论数据上进行强化学习和监督微调实验，评估性能提升及向其他物理领域的泛化能力，并分析微调前后推理链中的错误演化。
limitations: 仅针对量子场论一个领域，泛化性需进一步验证。
模型规模限于7B参数，更大模型效果未知。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.reason: 首次针对理论物理微调小推理模型，方法创新且开源数据，但实验细节有限。
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 首个针对量子场论微调小推理模型的研究，开发数据生成管线并分析推理错误演化。

papers.title: AutomationBench
papers.abstract: Existing AI benchmarks for software automation rarely combine cross-application coordination, autonomous API discovery, and policy adherence. Real business workflows demand all three: a single task may span a CRM, inbox, calendar, and messaging platform - requiring the agent to find the right endpoints, follow a policy document, and write correct data to each system. To address this gap, we introduce AutomationBench, a benchmark for evaluating AI agents on cross-application workflow orchestration via REST APIs. Drawing on real workflow patterns from Zapier's platform, tasks span Sales, Marketing, Operations, Support, Finance, and HR domains. Agents must discover relevant endpoints themselves, follow layered business rules, and navigate environments with irrelevant and sometimes misleading records. Grading is programmatic and end-state only: whether the correct data ended up in the right systems. Even the best frontier models currently score below 10%. AutomationBench provides a challenging, realistic measure of where current models stand relative to the agentic capabilities businesses actually need.

analysis.tldr: AutomationBench 是一个评估AI代理跨应用工作流编排能力的基准，当前最强模型得分低于10%。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有软件自动化基准缺乏跨应用协调、自主API发现和策略遵循的组合评估，而真实业务工作流需要这些能力。
analysis.problem: 缺乏一个能同时评估跨应用协调、自主API发现和策略遵循的基准。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 即使最强的前沿模型在AutomationBench上得分也低于10%，表明当前模型在真实业务所需的代理能力上存在显著差距。
analysis.method: 基于Zapier平台的真实工作流模式，构建跨Sales、Marketing等6个领域的任务，要求代理自主发现端点、遵循分层业务规则，并处理无关或误导性记录，通过最终状态进行程序化评分。
contributions: 提出了AutomationBench，一个评估AI代理跨应用工作流编排能力的基准。
揭示了当前最强模型在真实业务场景下的性能瓶颈（得分低于10%）。
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果，仅提及最强模型得分低于10%。
limitations: 基准可能无法覆盖所有真实业务场景的复杂性。
仅通过最终状态评分，可能忽略中间过程的合理性。
analysis.primary_area_en: datasets and benchmarks
analysis.reason: 基准设计新颖且针对实际业务需求，但摘要未提供详细实验数据，适合精读。
analysis.one_sentence_summary: AutomationBench 是一个评估AI代理跨应用工作流编排能力的基准，当前最强模型得分低于10%。

papers.title: Gated Memory Policy
papers.abstract: Robotic manipulation tasks exhibit varying memory requirements, ranging from Markovian tasks that require no memory to non-Markovian tasks that depend on historical information spanning single or multiple interaction trials. Surprisingly, simply extending observation histories of a visuomotor policy often leads to a significant performance drop due to distribution shift and overfitting. To address these issues, we propose Gated Memory Policy (GMP), a visuomotor policy that learns both when to recall memory and what to recall. To learn when to recall memory, GMP employs a learned memory gate mechanism that selectively activates history context only when necessary, improving robustness and reactivity. To learn what to recall efficiently, GMP introduces a lightweight cross-attention module that constructs effective latent memory representations. To further enhance robustness, GMP injects diffusion noise into historical actions, mitigating sensitivity to noisy or inaccurate histories during both training and inference. On our proposed non-Markovian benchmark MemMimic, GMP achieves a 30.1% average success rate improvement over long-history baselines, while maintaining competitive performance on Markovian tasks in RoboMimic. All code, data and in-the-wild deployment instructions are available on our project website https://gated-memory-policy.github.io/.

analysis.tldr: 提出Gated Memory Policy，通过门控记忆机制和轻量交叉注意力解决视觉运动策略中长历史观测导致的分布偏移和过拟合问题。
analysis.research_motivation: 机器人操作任务中，不同任务对记忆需求不同，但简单扩展观测历史会导致分布偏移和过拟合，性能显著下降。
analysis.problem: 如何让视觉运动策略在需要记忆的非马尔可夫任务中有效利用历史信息，同时避免因历史扩展带来的鲁棒性和过拟合问题。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 简单扩展观测历史会导致分布偏移和过拟合，性能显著下降。
analysis.method: 提出Gated Memory Policy (GMP)，包含三个关键组件：1) 学习型记忆门控机制，选择性激活历史上下文；2) 轻量交叉注意力模块，构建有效潜在记忆表征；3) 向历史动作注入扩散噪声，增强对噪声历史的鲁棒性。
contributions: 提出Gated Memory Policy，通过门控记忆和交叉注意力实现选择性记忆回忆。
引入扩散噪声注入历史动作，提升对噪声历史的鲁棒性。
构建非马尔可夫基准MemMimic，并在其上实现30.1%的平均成功率提升。
analysis.experiments: 在非马尔可夫基准MemMimic上，GMP相比长历史基线平均成功率提升30.1%；在RoboMimic的马尔可夫任务上保持竞争性能。
limitations: 可能对门控机制的训练稳定性敏感。
扩散噪声注入可能影响历史信息的精确性。
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to robotics, autonomy, planning

papers.title: Tadabur: A Large-Scale Quran Audio Dataset
papers.abstract: Despite growing interest in Quranic data research, existing Quran datasets remain limited in both scale and diversity. To address this gap, we present Tadabur, a large-scale Quran audio dataset. Tadabur comprises more than 1400+ hours of recitation audio from over 600 distinct reciters, providing substantial variation in recitation styles, vocal characteristics, and recording conditions. This diversity makes Tadabur a comprehensive and representative resource for Quranic speech research and analysis. By significantly expanding both the total duration and variability of available Quran data, Tadabur aims to support future research and facilitate the development of standardized Quranic speech benchmarks.

analysis.tldr: Tadabur 是一个包含 1400+ 小时、600+ 位诵经师的大型古兰经音频数据集，旨在填补现有数据集在规模和多样性上的空白。

analysis.problem: 缺乏大规模、多样化的古兰经音频数据集，以促进古兰经语音研究和标准化基准的开发。

contributions: 构建了目前最大规模、最多样化的古兰经音频数据集 Tadabur。
为古兰经语音研究和标准化基准开发提供了重要资源。
